article_meta.title: A copy of the Ten Commandments is posted along with other historical documents in a hallway of the Georgia Capitol on June 20, 2024, in Atlanta

reasoning: The model committed multiple core errors: it flagged quoted/source-attributed text as author bias twice, used non-approved bias type labels, and its one non-quoted example is a weak/unsupported bias claim. It also missed any subtle author-level bias that might exist. Overall, severe attribution and labeling failures.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Two flagged passages are direct quotes from a judge and an ACLU spokesperson; these must not be treated as the author's bias.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three items are not genuine author-level bias: two are quoted sources; the remaining item is largely a neutral factual attribution to Republicans.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: If any author-level bias exists, it is subtle (e.g., phrasing like 'to incorporate religion in public schools'). The model did not capture such nuances, but major omissions are not evident.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels used ('Political Bias', 'Source Bias', 'Loaded Language') are not from the allowed set, and the attributions are misapplied to quotes rather than author prose.
